text: Midã kɔvɔlabi, kɔádu funitsã, kɔákpɔta funitsã kako nɛ tili kamɛ, lɛmɛ mi Ɔlɛga nɛ ode a adɔɔla koe! Mbɔɛɛ mitátɛ kɔya miba kɔvɔlabi?
translated: Midã is a kɔvɔlabi, a kɔádu person, a kɔákpɔta person, just like the tili kamɛ, the mother of Ɔlɛga, who is the mother of all koe! What then is the difference between a kɔvɔlabi and a kɔvɔlabi?

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit. You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces.
text: “Fɔmɔ aana kutse libi wĩ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ ana kutse wĩ, ade ntɛ odeĩ kutse kpa ni, kutse libi kpa aana. Babi a kĩ kutse awũ a bakɔa kanyi kutse a odu.
translated: Fɔmɔ works well on the task, and he also works on it, and even though he is tired, the task is done. Babi is in the house and in the farm.

ref: Many will give up and will betray and hate each other.
text: Bati pii aamuniki nɛ be katɛkado liti. Baanyɛ be akũ akũ abɔa, bikesĩ akũ lɛmɛ.
translated: Bati went to the farm and ate yam. Baanyɛ and his children went to the river.

ref: But in front of everyone Peter said, “That isn't so! I don't know what you are talking about!”
text: Lɛmɛ Petro sĩ nɛ bati a flee anɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ĩnyina lɛtsama nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ alɛ a akũ.”
translated: "And when the rooster crowed, Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said to him, 'Come back and strengthen your brothers.'"

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: ade ebi ye kĩ, “Yidza, kakpã obisɛ̃ a mɔ ɔma mimuniki minaa Israelfɔ tite akũ, kitonɛkĩ bikĩ bɛwɛ kĩ bakɔ obisɛ̃ a ku.”
translated: And he said, "Lord, you have indeed chosen the children of Israel to be your people, and you have made a covenant with them."

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?

translated: Midã works, eats food, drinks water, and with clean hands, then my God, Ode, praise to the people, koe! How can I stop working?
